results.title: UM10204 I2C-Bus Specification and User Manual
results.description: NXP Semiconductors developed a simple bidirectional 2-wire bus for efficient inter-IC control. This bus is called the Inter-IC or I2C-bus.
results.content:
NXP Semiconductors developed a simple bidirectional 2-wire bus for efficient inter-IC control. This bus is called the Inter-IC or I2C-bus.

results.title: UM10204 I2C-Bus Specification and User Manual | PDF | Bit Rate | Bit
results.description: Timing parameters are independent for capacitive load up to 100 pF for each bus line allowing the maximum possible bit rate of 3.4 Mbit/s. At a higher capacitive load on the bus lines, the bit rate decreases gradually.
results.content:
Timing parameters are independent for capacitive load up to 100 pF for each bus line allowing the maximum possible bit rate of 3.4 Mbit/s. At a higher capacitive load on the bus lines, the bit rate decreases gradually.
This will automatically be the case if the User manual UM10204 · NXP Semiconductors device does not stretch the LOW period of the SCL signal. If such a device does stretch the LOW period of the SCL signal, it must output the next data bit to the SDA line tr(max) + tSU;DAT = 1000 + 250 = 1250 ns (according to the Standard-mode I2C-bus specification) before the SCL line is released. Also the acknowledge timing must meet this set-up time.

Timing parameters are independent for capacitive load up to 100 pF for each bus line allowing the maximum possible bit rate of 3.4 Mbit/s. At a higher capacitive load on the bus lines, the bit rate decreases gradually. The timing parameters for a capacitive bus load of 400 pF are specified in Table 12, allowing a maximum bit rate of 1.7 Mbit/s. results.title: UM10204 - NXP Semiconductors
results.description: Serial, 8-bit oriented, bidirectional data transfers can be made at up to 100 kbit/s in the Standard-mode, up to 400 kbit/s in the Fast-mode, up to 1 Mbit/s in the Fast-mode Plus (Fm+), or up to 3.4 Mbit/s in the High-speed mode.
results.content: Serial, 8-bit oriented, bidirectional data transfers can be made at up to 100 kbit/s in the Standard-mode, up to 400 kbit/s in the Fast-mode, up to 1 Mbit/s in the Fast-mode Plus (Fm+), or up to 3.4 Mbit/s in the High-speed mode. UM10204 I 2 C-bus specification and user manual Rev. 5 — 9 October 2012 User manual Document information Info Content Keywords I2C, I2C-bus, Standard-mode, Fast-mode, Fast-mode Plus, Fm+, Ultra Fast-mode, UFm, High Speed, Hs, inter-IC, SDA, SCL, USDA, USCL Abstract Philips Semiconductors (now NXP Semiconductors) developed a simple bidirectional 2-wire bus for efficient inter-IC control. This bus is called the Inter-IC or I2C-bus. Only two bus lines are required: a serial data line (SDA) and a serial clock line (SCL). Serial, 8-bit oriented, bidirectional data transfers can be made at up to 100 kbit/s in the Standard-mode, up to 400 kbit/s in the Fast-mode, up to 1 Mbit/s in the Fast-mode Plus (Fm+), or up to 3.4 Mbit/s in the High-speed mode. The Ultra Fast-mode is a uni-directional mode with data transfers of up to 5 Mbit/s. ... Page 18 and 19: NXP Semiconductors UM10204 Fig 16. Page 22 and 23: NXP Semiconductors UM10204 Table 4. Page 32 and 33: NXP Semiconductors UM10204 4.

results.description: © NXP Semiconductors N.V. 2014. All rights reserved. ... Rev. 6 — 4 April 2014 ... I2C-bus protocol. The ACK and NACK are also referred to as the ninth clock cycle. The · master generates all clock pulses, including the ninth clock pulse. The ninth data bit is · always driven HIGH (‘1’). Slave devices are not allowed to drive the SDA line at any time.
